message.content: Adversaries may exploit Microsoft Outlook's Home Page feature to gain persistence on compromised systems. This legacy feature allows customization of Outlook folders by loading internal or external URLs. Attackers can craft malicious HTML pages that execute code when loaded by Outlook Home Page. Once these malicious pages are added to a user's mailbox, they activate whenever the relevant Outlook folder is opened or reloaded. Notably, the OilRig group has leveraged this technique for persistence, even using CVEs to bypass initial patches designed to mitigate this abuse. Tools like Ruler can automate the exploitation of Outlook Home Pages. The potential impact includes unauthorized code execution and persistent access to the compromised system. Mitigation strategies involve disabling the Outlook Home Page feature, applying security patches promptly, and monitoring for unusual changes to Outlook settings.
